goal: Provide practical, evidence-based relaxation techniques to help with middle-of-the-night wake-ups, including breathing exercises, progressive muscle relaxation, and guidance on exercise timing and impact.
Provide practical pacing guidance for shifting bedtime (earlier or later) with weekly increments (e.g., 15 minutes per day or 15–30 minutes per week).
Prioritize routines as the starting point for improving sleep, including a consistent wake time and a reliable wind-down and stimulus-control rules.
Maximize restorative sleep quality within limited time in bed by focusing on sleep efficiency, sleep depth, and circadian alignment.
Provide affordable, evidence-based lumbar support options (toppers and pillows) that balance cost and effectiveness.
Create a drug-free sleep plan tailored to a 5:15 AM wake time, subway noise, and low-back pain.
constraints: Noise-reduction strategies must not compromise alarm wake-up reliability; include audible and/or tactile wake-up backups.
Space and budget are limited; prioritize compact, low-cost, landlord-friendly options that do not require major structural changes.
Back health considerations: avoid stomach sleeping; prefer side sleeping with knee support and lumbar support.
Mattress firmness guidance for back pain: aim for medium-firm to firm; avoid overly soft toppers; consider landlord-friendly options like firmer toppers or plywood base if needed.
Avoid prescription or nightly OTC sleep medications.
decisions: Ensure alarm reliability when advising on noise reduction by recommending vibration backups, multiple alarms, and audible redundancies.
entities: Knee pillow (crescent or wedge) 4–6 inch thick
Adjustable head pillow with removable fill
Plywood under mattress (1/2–3/4 inch) for trial
30-day return policy on toppers and pillows
open: Provide a short shopping list tailored to budget (earplug types, under-pillow alarm styles, masker devices or apps)
